explanation: Diisopropyl sebacate is a lightweight synthetic ester emollient derived from sebacic acid and isopropyl alcohol, providing a dry, non-greasy skin feel with excellent spreadability. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used as a skin-feel enhancer in sunscreens and lightweight moisturizers.

Diisostearyl malate is a rich emollient ester from isostearic acid and malic acid with a comfortable, non-sticky feel, widely used in lip products and anhydrous formulas. It provides excellent adhesion and gloss without greasiness, and is well-tolerated by most skin types.

Dilauryl thiodipropionate is a synthetic antioxidant preservative used at very low concentrations in skincare to prevent the oxidative degradation of fats and oils in a formula. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels and functions as a formula stabilizer rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Dimer dilinoleyl dimer dilinoleate is a polyfunctional ester derived from dilinoleic acid (from linoleic acid dimerization), used as a rich, non-greasy emollient and film-former in lip products and anhydrous formulas. It provides excellent skin adhesion and a cushioned feel, and is considered well-tolerated with low comedogenic potential.

Dimethicone is a polydimethylsiloxane — the most widely used silicone in skincare — that forms a breathable, protective film on skin, reduces transepidermal water loss, and imparts a characteristic silky, slip-enhancing texture. It is non-sensitizing, non-comedogenic, and exceptionally well-tolerated, making it one of the most skin-compatible ingredients available across all skin types.

Dimethicone/PEG-10/15 crosspolymer is a PEG-modified crosslinked dimethicone used as a silicone emulsifier and sensory modifier in water-in-silicone formulas, contributing a smooth, lightweight texture. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Dimethicone/silsesquioxane copolymer is a hybrid silicone that combines the smoothing properties of dimethicone with the film-forming and soft-focus properties of silsesquioxane resin. It is used in primers and foundations for its ability to blur pores and fine lines, and is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Dimethicone/vinyl dimethicone crosspolymer is a crosslinked silicone elastomer used as a texture modifier and sensory agent in primers, sunscreens, and lightweight moisturizers, providing a bouncy, velvety skin feel. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and creates a soft-focus effect without heavy or occlusive residue.

Dimethiconol is a hydroxyl-terminated silicone polymer closely related to dimethicone, used as a conditioning agent and film-former in skin and hair products. It provides slightly more substantivity than standard dimethicone, helping to coat and protect the surface, and is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Dimethiconol fluoroalcohol dilinoleic acid is a fluorinated silicone derivative used as a water- and oil-repellent film-former in long-wear cosmetics. The trailing period in this ingredient listing suggests it may be a data artifact from label parsing. It is considered well-tolerated at typical use concentrations.

Dimethyl sulfone (MSM, methylsulfonylmethane) is an organic sulfur compound with anti-inflammatory and mild antioxidant properties, used in skincare to soothe irritation and support collagen synthesis. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating, and is included in calming and firming formulas for its ability to reduce inflammatory mediators and support connective tissue.

Dioctyl sodium sulfosuccinate (docusate sodium) is a mild anionic surfactant and wetting agent used in cleansers and emulsions to improve the spreading and penetration of formula components. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations and is significantly gentler than sulfate surfactants.

Dioscorea japonica (Japanese wild yam) root extract contains diosgenin and polysaccharides with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties, used in anti-aging formulas for its ability to support skin firmness and protect against oxidative stress. It is well-tolerated and is commonly used in Japanese skincare for its soothing and nourishing benefits.

Dipentaerythrityl tetrahydroxystearate/tetraisostearate is a complex polyfunctional ester emollient derived from pentaerythritol and hydroxystearic/isostearic fatty acids, providing a rich, non-greasy skin feel and excellent film-forming properties in luxurious moisturizers and lip products. It is well-tolerated and non-comedogenic.

Diphenylsiloxy phenyl trimethicone is a phenyl-modified silicone with a higher refractive index than standard dimethicone, used in foundation and skin care formulas to enhance gloss, optical radiance, and a luminous skin finish. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and provides excellent spreadability.

Dipotassium glycyrrhizate is the potassium salt of glycyrrhizic acid extracted from licorice root, widely recognized as one of the most effective botanical anti-inflammatory actives in skincare for its ability to inhibit inflammatory prostaglandins and reduce redness. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-irritating, and is used in calming and brightening formulas for reactive, rosacea-prone, and post-procedure skin.

Dipotassium phosphate is an inorganic potassium salt used in skincare as a pH buffer to stabilize formula acidity and protect active ingredients. It is considered inert and well-tolerated at typical use concentrations.

Dipropylene glycol is a diol solvent and humectant used in skincare to dissolve fragrance and other ingredients while providing mild moisture-retention. It is larger and less irritating than propylene glycol, and is considered well-tolerated by most skin types including sensitive at typical formula concentrations.

Disodium cocoamphodiacetate is a mild amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil and amino acids, used in gentle cleansers and shampoos as a secondary surfactant that conditions while cleansing and reduces the irritation potential of primary surfactants. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is commonly found in tear-free and low-irritation formulas.

Disodium cocoyl glutamate is a mild amino acid-derived surfactant from coconut fatty acids and glutamic acid, used in gentle cleansers for effective cleansing at a skin-compatible slightly acidic pH. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, biodegradable, and among the gentlest surfactants available — widely used in sensitive-skin and natural-positioned cleansers.

Disodium EDTA is a chelating agent that binds calcium, magnesium, and iron ions in a formula to prevent rancidity, discoloration, and preservative deactivation caused by trace metals. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations — its function is formula protection rather than skin activity.

Disodium EDTA is a chelating agent that binds trace metal ions in a formula to prevent oxidative degradation, discoloration, and preservative failure. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Disodium laureth sulfosuccinate is a mild anionic surfactant derived from lauryl alcohol and succinic acid, used in gentle cleansers as a softer alternative to sodium lauryl sulfate. It is considerably less irritating than standard sulfate surfactants and is well-tolerated by sensitive skin in rinse-off formulas.

Disodium stearoyl glutamate is an amino acid-derived emulsifier from stearic acid and glutamic acid, used in oil-in-water emulsions to create stable, skin-compatible formulas with a smooth, lightweight texture. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and favored in natural and clean-formula emulsions for its skin-compatible chemistry.

Disteardimonium hectorite is a quaternary ammonium-treated clay mineral used as a thickener and suspension agent in anhydrous and oil-phase formulas such as foundations, lip products, and sunscreens. It is considered inert and well-tolerated, providing viscosity and stability to formulas without significant sensitization risk.

DMDM hydantoin is a formaldehyde-releasing preservative that slowly releases formaldehyde in a formula to inhibit microbial growth. Formaldehyde is a well-documented contact allergen and sensitizer — DMDM hydantoin can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or sensitive skin with repeated exposure, and is increasingly avoided in clean-formula skincare.

Drometrizole trisiloxane is a photostable UV filter that absorbs both UVA and UVB radiation without degrading in sunlight, and is approved in the EU, Japan, and Canada but not yet in the US. It is well-tolerated and considered less sensitizing than some other chemical UV filters, making it a useful broad-spectrum filter in international formulations.

Eclipta prostrata (false daisy) extract is a traditional Ayurvedic botanical rich in wedelolactone and coumestans with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and calming formulas for its ability to protect skin from oxidative stress.

Eclipta prostrata leaf extract is derived from the leaf of the false daisy plant, providing wedelolactone and coumestan antioxidants with anti-inflammatory and skin-brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and calming formulas for its antioxidant content.

EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid) is a chelating agent that binds metal ions in skincare formulas to prevent oxidative degradation, discoloration, and preservative deactivation caused by trace minerals. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations — its function is formula stability rather than skin activity.
